title: leucopogon darlingensis subsp. rectus
intro: Leucopogon darlingensis subsp. rectus, also known as the Rectus Beard Heath, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is valued for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The leaves of the Leucopogon darlingensis subsp. rectus plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a natural remedy for colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
